stories.title: CPSC Water Safety Alert — "Relaxing Baby" Swim Float Linked to Toddler Death; Baby Bath Seats Recalled [TIER 1]

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: ⚠️ CPSC urgent warning: "Relaxing Baby" swim floats for infants/toddlers linked to 1 child death. Floats can flip over and submerge your child. Sold on Amazon + eBay. Stop use now — puncture and discard. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Recalled: YCXXKJ baby bath seats (sold on Amazon by BenTalk). Nearly 9,000 recalled — seats can tip over, creating a drowning risk. Colors: blue, gray, pink, yellow. Email bentalkhere@outlook.com for a full refund. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
COS parents, two water product alerts you need to know about before spring really gets going. 💧

The most urgent: the CPSC issued a warning on April 2nd to immediately stop using "Relaxing Baby" branded swim floats for infants and toddlers. One child — a 2-year-old — died. The floats can flip over and submerge a child underwater. The manufacturer based in China has not cooperated with the CPSC, which means there's no formal refund program. The action is simple but serious: puncture the float so it can never be inflated again, then throw it away. If you bought this product on Amazon or eBay and it's sitting in a garage waiting for summer, please check it now.

The second recall is the YCXXKJ baby bath seat sold on Amazon by a seller called BenTalk. About 8,960 of these seats have been recalled because they can tip over during bath time — a drowning risk. They come in blue, gray, pink, and yellow, with little duck, turtle, or whale toys and four suction cups on the bottom. If you have one, stop using it. You can get a full refund by writing "Recalled" on the front in permanent marker, photographing it, and emailing two photos to bentalkhere@outlook.com.

Water safety matters year-round, but especially as we head into the warmer months. Please share this post with anyone who might need it. 🙏

Have you checked the CPSC.gov recalls page recently?
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption -- 8:30 AM MT

```
CPSC water safety alert: "Relaxing Baby" swim floats linked to 1 toddler death. Plus: YCXXKJ bath seats recalled. Check both now.
```

stories.title: AAP Breaks with CDC on Childhood Vaccines — What COS Parents Need to Know [TIER 1]

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text: The AAP published its own 2026 childhood vaccine schedule — recommending 18 diseases vs. the CDC's revised 11. A federal judge has since blocked the CDC changes. Here's what to ask your COS pediatrician. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
If you've been hearing confusing things about childhood vaccines lately — you're not imagining it. Here's what's actually going on, in plain language.

The American Academy of Pediatrics (AAP) has published its own 2026 Recommended Childhood and Adolescent Immunization Schedule in the journal Pediatrics. It recommends vaccination against 18 diseases. That puts it at odds with the revised CDC schedule (shaped by RFK Jr.'s restructuring of the vaccine advisory committee), which moved to recommending only 11 routine vaccinations. Six vaccines — Hepatitis A, Hepatitis B, rotavirus, flu/influenza, RSV, and meningococcal disease — were moved from "routine" recommendations to something called "shared clinical decision-making." The AAP has called those changes "dangerous and unnecessary."

Here's where it stands legally: on March 16, a U.S. District Court judge blocked the CDC's new vaccine schedule, ruling that the restructured advisory committee was "unlawfully constituted" and that its decisions were "arbitrary and capricious." The June 2024 CDC schedule has been restored while litigation continues. The AAP was one of the plaintiffs in that case.

What does this mean for your family? The most practical thing you can do is ask your pediatrician which schedule they're following and whether any of your child's upcoming vaccines might be affected. Most AAP-member pediatricians will follow the AAP's broader schedule. Insurance coverage for some vaccines may also be in flux — worth a direct call to your provider.

This is genuinely confusing, and we know it. We're here to help cut through the noise.

What questions do you have about the vaccine schedule changes?
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption -- 10:30 AM MT

```
AAP vs. CDC: two different 2026 childhood vaccine schedules. A judge blocked the CDC changes. Here's what to ask your pediatrician.
```

stories.title: D49 Budget Crisis — $2M Accounting Error, Teacher Layoffs, and What Comes Next [TIER 2]

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text: D49 parents: the district is cutting 50 positions after a $9–11M budget shortfall — and a KRDO investigation found administrators made a $2M accounting error before $1.8M in teacher salary cuts. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text: D49 is cutting positions — but also opening 2 new full-day preschool classrooms at the Student Success Center this August. One district, two very different stories playing out at the same time. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
If your kids are in District 49 — or you're a D49 teacher or staff member — this is a story worth understanding fully. 🏫

D49 declared fiscal exigency in January 2026. That's a formal declaration of financial emergency. The district is navigating a $9–11 million budget gap driven by declining enrollment, the defunding of mill levy equalization, rising healthcare costs, and inflation. The board approved cutting approximately 50 positions — teachers and administrators.

But a KRDO investigative report from March adds an important layer. KRDO found that D49 administrators made a $2 million accounting error in a state scheduling audit — and paid back $2,040,128.86 to the state in June 2025. Shortly after, the district proceeded with $1.8 million in teacher salary cuts. The teachers' union publicly challenged the sequencing.

We're not here to take sides — but if you're a D49 parent trying to understand why 50 positions are being cut and what the full picture looks like, the KRDO investigation is worth reading. The Gazette and Rocky Mountain PBS have also covered the budget crisis in depth.

Here's the fuller picture: D49 is still planning to open two new full-day preschool classrooms at the D49 Student Success Center this August. Budget cuts and program expansion are happening simultaneously. That's the complexity of running a school district in 2026.

What questions do you have about the D49 budget situation?
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption -- 12:30 PM MT

```
D49 is cutting 50 positions after a $9–11M budget crisis. A KRDO investigation found a $2M accounting error before $1.8M in teacher salary cuts.
```

stories.title: COS Spring Family Events — Bear Creek Toddler Class, Wickedly Broadway, Star Wars Live [TIER 2]

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: COS April events — Bear Creek toddler nature class (Apr 10, ages 2–3, $3), Wickedly Broadway at Pikes Peak Center (Apr 10–11), and FREE ASL literacy event at Bear Creek (Apr 17, ages birth–8). #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
Easter is behind us. Now let's talk about what's coming up in Colorado Springs this spring. 🌸

If you have a toddler (ages 2–3), the Bear Creek Nature Center is hosting a Toddler Nature Class at Fountain Creek Nature Center on April 10. There are two sessions — 10 to 11:15 a.m. and 2 to 3:15 p.m. — and tickets are just $3 per person. It's a great low-key introduction to the outdoors for the youngest kids in the family.

Later that week, April 10–11, the Colorado Springs Philharmonic brings "Wickedly Broadway" to Pikes Peak Center. Think villain anthems and musical theater hits from Wicked, Phantom of the Opera, and more — a great outing for kids who love musicals and an excuse for parents to enjoy live music.

The standout free event of the month is April 17 at Bear Creek Nature Center. The Colorado School for the Deaf and the Blind (CSDB) is co-hosting a free early childhood literacy event for ages birth–8. Families will read a book together in American Sign Language, learn about local wildlife, and go on a nature walk. It's a beautiful, inclusive event and it costs nothing. Clear your calendar.

And looking ahead to May: the Colorado Springs Philharmonic presents Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back with John Williams' score performed live while the film plays on the big screen. May 8–9 at Pikes Peak Center. If you have a Star Wars fan in the house, this one's worth planning ahead for.

Which of these are you adding to your calendar?
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption -- 3:30 PM MT

```
COS spring events: toddler nature class, Wickedly Broadway, FREE ASL literacy event, and Star Wars live. April has a lot going on.
```

stories.title: Rock Island Trail Gets New Bridge + April Is Child Abuse Prevention Month [TIER 3]

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: April is Child Abuse Prevention Month. COS families: the Childhelp National Child Abuse Hotline is 1-800-422-4453. And the Rock Island Trail is getting a new 210-ft pedestrian bridge this spring. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
Two things worth knowing as April gets underway in Colorado Springs.

First, the good news for families who use the Rock Island Trail: the City of Colorado Springs is launching construction this spring on a 210-foot pedestrian bridge over Sand Creek. The bridge will connect the east and west sides of the trail, which currently requires navigating a gap in the trail system. The project also includes a new pedestrian-activated flashing crossing at Peterson Road and more than 2 miles of new trails. Construction is expected to take 10 to 12 months — so there may be some disruption near the Sand Creek crossing area while work is underway. This project is funded by PPRTA II, CDOT grants, and the TOPS sales tax that COS voters approved. It's a meaningful trail upgrade for families who bike, walk, and run this corridor.

Second: April is Child Abuse Prevention Month. We don't always love talking about hard things, but this one matters. If you or someone you know needs to report a concern or find support, the Childhelp National Child Abuse Hotline is available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week at 1-800-422-4453. Children's Hospital Colorado is also an active resource for Colorado Springs families dealing with difficult situations.

This is also a good month to have conversations with your kids about body safety, trusted adults, and how to speak up when something doesn't feel right. Colorado Springs has good resources. You don't have to navigate these conversations alone.

What resources do you wish you'd had earlier as a parent?
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption -- 8:30 PM MT

```
Rock Island Trail is getting a new 210-ft pedestrian bridge this spring. Plus: April is Child Abuse Prevention Month — Childhelp hotline: 1-800-422-4453.
```
